_comment: Tunable parameters for the Layer 1 breeding-favorability formula. Change these and re-run; no code edits needed. See README.md > Methodology for the rationale behind each number.

windows._comment: Day windows are measured in days-ago from the latest available observation (age 0 = most recent day).

temperature._comment: Suitability is 0 below min_c, ramps to 1 across [min_c, opt_low_c], holds 1 across the optimal band, ramps back to 0 across [opt_high_c, max_c], and is 0 above max_c. Aedes/Anopheles breed fastest ~25-30C.

humidity._comment: Relative humidity favorability: 0 at/below low_pct, ramps to 1 at/above high_pct. RH >~60% extends mosquito lifespan and biting activity.

rainfall._comment: v2 (L1-0.2.0) rainfall suitability: S_R(R) = (1 - exp(-R/scale_mm)) * heavy-rain penalty, applied to both the recent and lagged rainfall totals. Moderate rain creates breeding habitats; rain above heavy_rain_threshold_mm is progressively discounted (flush-out effect), so the response is non-monotonic.

weights._comment: Weights for the in-temperature favorability blend. MUST sum to 1.0. Lagged rain carries the most weight because emergence lags rain by ~1-2 weeks. Temperature is applied separately as a limiting multiplier (see README).

buckets._comment: Lower bounds for each category on the 0-100 score. score < moderate = Low; [moderate, high) = Moderate; [high, very_high) = High; >= very_high = Very High.

sanity._comment: Data-quality guard rails. The build aborts (writes nothing) if more than max_fail_fraction of cities fail to score, and flags any city whose mean temperature falls outside [plausible_temp_min_c, plausible_temp_max_c].
